title: Fix static initialization order for packages with class hierarchies
body:
## Summary

This patch fixes package static initialization ordering.  Level-based
module sorting can place an importing package before the imported one in
`_eval_static`, causing the imported package's state to be reset after
the importer has already triggered side effects in it.

A gap found in CVV integration: https://github.com/openhwgroup/core-v-verif/pull/2724

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
package pkg_a;
  int g_state = 0;
  class svc_t;
    local static svc_t inst;
    static function svc_t get();
      if (inst == null) begin inst = new(); g_state = 1; end
      return inst;
    endfunction
  endclass
endpackage

package pkg_b;
  import pkg_a::*;
  class comp_c;
    function new(); svc_t s = pkg_a::svc_t::get(); endfunction
  endclass
  comp_c obj = new();  // triggers pkg_a::svc_t::get() during static init
endpackage

module t;
  initial begin
    pkg_a::svc_t s = pkg_a::svc_t::get();
    if (pkg_a::g_state != 1) $stop;  // g_state was reset to 0
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```

On master: `$stop` -- `pkg_b` eval_static runs before `pkg_a`, so
`g_state` is set to 1 by `get()`, then reset to 0 by `pkg_a`'s
initializer.  At runtime `get()` sees `inst != null` and skips the
`g_state = 1` assignment.

## Changes

- `src/V3Sched.cpp` (`createStatic`): Re-sort `m_static` entries so
  `AstPackage` modules are ordered by source file position, restoring
  compilation order (IEEE 1800-2023 26.3).

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_static_init_pkg_order.{v,py}`

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
